title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: lwdidR R implementation Lee Wooldridge (2025) panel difference--differences estimator. estimator first transforms outcomes using unit’s pre-treatment observations, estimates treatment effect cross section.

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: lwdidR implements estimator : Lee, Y., & Wooldridge, J. M. (2025). simple panel data approach difference--differences general treatment patterns. key idea simple: residualize unit’s outcome using pre-treatment observations, either demeaning detrending, run pooled cross-sectional OLS. package supports: - Common-timing staggered adoption designs - Transformation methods: demean, detrend, demeanq, detrendq - Standard errors: homoskedastic OLS, HC1, HC3, cluster-robust - Cohort-specific ATTs (g,r)-level event-study estimates

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
code: library(lwdidR)  # Load bundled Castle Doctrine dataset (50 US states, 2000-2010) castle <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "castle.csv", package = "lwdidR")) castle$gvar <- castle$effyear castle$gvar[is.na(castle$gvar) | castle$gvar == 0] <- NA  # Staggered design: demeaning with HC3 SEs res <- lwdid(castle, "lhomicide", "sid", "year",              gvar = "gvar", rolling = "demean", vce = "hc3") print(res) Lee-Wooldridge DiD (lwdidR) Design:      staggered Transf.:     demean VCE:         HC3 -------------------------------------------------- Overall ATT:   0.0917 SE:            0.0612 t-stat:        1.4995 p-value:       0.1376

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: Castle Doctrine laws log homicide rates: results match paper Table 7.2 within tolerance 0.001. See vignette("castle_law") full replication.

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: Full documentation: https://xiangao.github.io/lwdidR/

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: Overall ATT (staggered): delta-method average cohort ATTs. Instead, pooled cross-sectional regression: - Treated unit : outcome = ydot_postavg cohort g - Never-treated unit : outcome = weighted average ydot_postavg across cohorts - Single OLS N_treated + N_never_treated units matches Python reference implementation yields SE ≈ 0.061 (0.051 naive averaging).

title: Lee-Wooldridge Difference-in-Differences via Unit-Specific Transformations
text: Lee, Y., & Wooldridge, J. M. (2025). simple panel data approach difference--differences general treatment patterns. Working paper.

what: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
text: Main entry point implementing Lee & Wooldridge (2025) panel difference--differences estimator. Supports common-timing staggered adoption designs. unit's outcome residualised using pre-treatment observations running pooled cross-sectional OLS.

title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
code: lwdid(   data,   y,   ivar,   tvar,   gvar = NULL,   post = NULL,   rolling = "demean",   control_group = "never_treated",   aggregate = "overall",   vce = NULL,   cluster_var = NULL,   controls = NULL,   season_var = NULL,   nboot = 999,   nperm = 999,   vce_inner = "hc3" )

title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
text: data long-format panel data frame (one row per unit-period). y Character. Name outcome column. ivar Character. Name unit identifier column. tvar Character. Name calendar time column (numeric integer). gvar Character NULL. Name first-treatment-year column staggered designs. Units value 0 NA treated never-treated. Set NULL (default) common-timing designs (supply post instead). post Character NULL. Name binary post-treatment indicator column (0 = pre, 1 = post). Required gvar = NULL; ignored otherwise. rolling Character. Transformation method applied unit's pre-treatment observations: "demean" Subtract unit's pre-period mean (default). "detrend" Remove linear trend fitted pre-periods. "demeanq" Seasonal demeaning; requires season_var. "detrendq" Seasonal detrending; requires season_var. control_group Character. Control group staggered designs: "never_treated" (default) "not_yet_treated". aggregate Character. Aggregation level staggered designs: "overall" (default), "cohort", "none" (returns (g,r) pairs). vce Character NULL. Variance-covariance estimator: NULL (homoskedastic OLS), "hc1", "hc3", "cluster", "wildboot" (wild cluster bootstrap), "permutation" (randomisation inference). last two distribution-free recommended small N. cluster_var Character NULL. Column name clustering; required vce = "cluster". controls Character vector NULL. Names time-invariant control variables include cross-sectional regression. season_var Character NULL. Column name seasonal indicator (required rolling = "demeanq" "detrendq"). nboot Integer. Number bootstrap replications vce = "wildboot" (default 999). nperm Integer. Number permutations vce = "permutation" (default 999). vce_inner Character. Inner variance estimator used computing observed t-statistic inside wild bootstrap (default "hc3").

title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
text: object class "lwdid", list containing: design "staggered" "common_timing". att_overall Estimated overall ATT. se_overall Standard error overall ATT. tstat t-statistic. pvalue Two-sided p-value. att_by_cohort Data frame cohort-specific ATTs (staggered ). att_by_cohort_time Data frame (g,r)-specific ATTs (staggered ). att_by_period Data frame period-specific ATTs (common timing ). ci_lower, ci_upper 95% confidence interval bounds (common timing ). N Sample size first post-treatment period (common timing ).

title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
text: Lee, Y., & Wooldridge, J. M. (2025). simple panel data approach difference--differences general treatment patterns.

title: Lee-Wooldridge DiD via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations — lwdid
code: # Load bundled Castle Doctrine dataset castle <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "castle.csv", package = "lwdidR")) castle$gvar <- castle$effyear castle$gvar[is.na(castle$gvar) | castle$gvar == 0] <- NA  # Staggered design with demeaning and HC3 standard errors res <- lwdid(castle, "lhomicide", "sid", "year",              gvar = "gvar", rolling = "demean", vce = "hc3") print(res) #>  #> Lee-Wooldridge DiD (lwdidR) #> Design:      staggered #> Transf.:     demean #> VCE:         HC3 #> -------------------------------------------------- #> Overall ATT:   0.0917 #> SE:            0.0612 #> t-stat:        1.4997 #> p-value:       0.1402 #> -------------------------------------------------- #>  #> Cohort-specific effects: #>  cohort     att      se tstat    pvalue #>    2005 0.08017 0.03215 2.493 1.884e-02 #>    2006 0.06824 0.08920 0.765 4.488e-01 #>    2007 0.11406 0.09838 1.159 2.552e-01 #>    2008 0.14605 0.08203 1.780 8.548e-02 #>    2009 0.21108 0.03550 5.946 2.115e-06 #>

what: LWDID for staggered adoption (gvar case) — lwdid_staggered
title: LWDID for staggered adoption (gvar case) — lwdid_staggered
text: Python-equivalent algorithm: cohort g: Apply transformation using pre-periods (t < g) units Compute ydot_postavg(g,) per unit Overall ATT: pooled regression Treated unit : Y_bar_i = ydot_postavg(g_i, ) Never-treated unit : Y_bar_i = sum_g w_g * ydot_postavg(g,) OLS: Y_bar ~ D (D=1 ever treated) N_treat + N_NT units Cohort ATT_g: per-cohort regression Y_bar(g,) = ydot_postavg(g,) cohort g units + NT controls OLS: Y_bar_g ~ D_g

title: LWDID for staggered adoption (gvar case) — lwdid_staggered
code: lwdid_staggered(   data,   y,   ivar,   tvar,   gvar,   rolling = "demean",   control_group = "never_treated",   aggregate = "overall",   vce = NULL,   cluster_var = NULL,   controls = NULL,   season_var = NULL,   nboot = 999,   nperm = 999,   vce_inner = "hc3" )

title: LWDID for staggered adoption (gvar case) — lwdid_staggered
text: data Long-format panel data y Outcome column ivar Unit identifier tvar Calendar time column gvar First treatment year (0/NA = never treated) rolling Transformation method control_group "never_treated" "not_yet_treated" aggregate "overall", "cohort", "none" vce Variance estimator cluster_var Cluster variable controls Control variable names season_var Seasonal indicator column

title: LWDID for staggered adoption (gvar case) — lwdid_staggered
text: Named list att_overall, att_by_cohort, att_by_cohort_time

title: Print an lwdid object — print.lwdid
text: Displays compact summary estimation results including design type, transformation method, VCE, overall ATT, SE, t-statistic, p-value. staggered designs also prints cohort-specific effects.

title: Print an lwdid object — print.lwdid
text: x object class "lwdid" returned lwdid(). ... Currently ignored.

title: Print an lwdid object — print.lwdid
text: Invisibly returns x.

title: Summarise an lwdid object — summary.lwdid
text: Calls print.lwdid() additionally prints period-specific ATTs (common-timing design) first 10 (g, r)-specific ATTs (staggered design).

title: lwdidR 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Implements Lee & Wooldridge (2025) estimator via unit-specific pre-treatment transformations Supports common-timing staggered adoption designs Transformation methods: demean, detrend, demeanq, detrendq Standard errors: homoskedastic OLS, HC1, HC3, cluster-robust (manual sandwich) Staggered design: overall pooled ATT, cohort-specific ATT, (g,r)-level effects Bundles Castle Doctrine dataset replication paper Section 7.2 Vignette replicating numerical results Lee & Wooldridge (2025) Table 7.2
